sections.heading: How do you add a Beacon to the Halo App?

pages.title: Dog Collar Feedback System - Halo 6-Type Training Cues

sections.heading: Feedback System
sections.subhead: Available on Halo Collar 5 · 6 Feedback Types · Cesar Millan Voice Commands · Static Always Optional
sections.copySummary: Patented Technology Halo Collar communicates with your dog through six types of feedback: three prevention and three encouragement. Each is fully customizable with sounds, vibration patterns, and optional static levels. The system is designed with Cesar Mil...

sections.heading: Feature overview
sections.subhead: Explore all Halo Collar features including GPS tracking, virtual fencing, and the Cesar Millan training program.
sections.copySummary: The Halo Collar Feedback System is the patented communication layer between the collar and your dog. It is designed around a principle from dog psychology: effective boundary training requires both prevention (telling the dog to stop) and encouragement (tel...
sections.notes: Nearby marker: container container--narrow No hosted asset candidates found in this section window.

sections.heading: Six Types of Intelligent Communication
sections.subhead: Halo’s feedback system is not a single correction. It is a layered communication system designed with Cesar Millan that guides your dog through escalating prevention cues and rewards correct behavior with encouragement.
sections.copySummary: How Feedback Works Halo’s feedback system is not a single correction. It is a layered communication system designed with Cesar Millan that guides your dog through escalating prevention cues and rewards correct behavior with encouragement.
sections.notes: Nearby marker: feature-grid__header text-center 1 hosted asset candidate(s).

sections.heading: Three Levels of Prevention Feedback
sections.copySummary: When your dog enters the warning area (approximately 7–10 feet from the fence boundary) and is actively moving toward the edge, the collar delivers Warning Feedback, the first and gentlest cue. If the dog ignores the warning and continues approaching, the c...
sections.notes: Nearby marker: feature-grid__card card carousel-item 2 hosted asset candidate(s).

sections.heading: Three Types of Encouragement Feedback
sections.copySummary: Encouragement is what makes Halo’s system fundamentally different from traditional shock collars. When your dog stops moving toward the boundary or turns back, the collar plays one of three positive cues: Whistle teaches your dog to return to you, “Good Dog...
sections.notes: Nearby marker: feature-grid__card card carousel-item 2 hosted asset candidate(s).

sections.heading: Direction-Aware Feedback Intelligence
sections.copySummary: The collar uses GPS position data and its built-in motion sensor to determine which direction your dog is facing and whether they are moving toward or away from the boundary. If the dog is already heading back to the safe zone, the collar does not escalate...
sections.notes: Nearby marker: feature-grid__card card carousel-item 2 hosted asset candidate(s).

sections.heading: Escape-Proof Patented Escalation
sections.copySummary: Halo’s patented feedback system does not give up. If your dog ignores all three prevention levels and crosses the boundary, the collar continues delivering Emergency Feedback throughout a Protection Zone that extends approximately 300 feet (91.4 meters) bey...
sections.notes: Nearby marker: feature-grid__card card carousel-item 2 hosted asset candidate(s).

sections.heading: Full Customization Through the Halo App
sections.copySummary: Every feedback type and intensity level is adjustable through the Halo app’s Feedback Settings on the Pet Card. For each of the three prevention levels (Warning, Boundary, Emergency), you choose independently between sound, vibration, or static — and set th...
sections.notes: Nearby marker: feature-grid__card card carousel-item 2 hosted asset candidate(s).

sections.heading: Manual Instant Feedback
sections.copySummary: In addition to automatic fence-triggered feedback, you can send any feedback type to your dog’s collar manually. The Halo app’s Instant Feedback screen lets you send a prevention or encouragement cue to the collar with a single tap delivered via Bluetooth (...
sections.notes: Nearby marker: feature-grid__card card carousel-item 1 hosted asset candidate(s).

sections.heading: From Warning to Encouragement: What Your Dog Experiences
sections.subhead: The following sequence illustrates what happens when a trained dog approaches, reaches, or crosses a Halo Fence boundary. Every step is designed so the dog can control the outcome through their own behavior.
sections.copySummary: Step by Step The following sequence illustrates what happens when a trained dog approaches, reaches, or crosses a Halo Fence boundary. Every step is designed so the dog can control the outcome through their own behavior. 1
sections.notes: Nearby marker: deep-dive__content No hosted asset candidates found in this section window.

sections.heading: Safe Zone
sections.subhead: Your dog is inside the fence boundary with no feedback active. The collar is tracking position via AlwaysOn™ GPS at 20 updates per second.
sections.copySummary: Your dog is inside the fence boundary with no feedback active. The collar is tracking position via AlwaysOn™ GPS at 20 updates per second. 2 <h3 class="deep-dive__step-label"
sections.notes: Nearby marker: deep-dive__step-content No hosted asset candidates found in this section window.

sections.heading: Warning Area (7–10 ft)
sections.copySummary: The dog enters the warning area and is moving toward the boundary. Warning Feedback activates — your configured sound, vibration, or static at its set intensity. This is the first cue your dog has been trained to recognize as “keep away.” 3
sections.notes: Nearby marker: deep-dive__step-content No hosted asset candidates found in this section window.

sections.heading: Continued Approach
sections.copySummary: If the dog ignores the warning and continues toward the boundary, Boundary Feedback is added simultaneously. If you have configured different modalities for Warning and Boundary (e.g., tone for Warning, vibration for Boundary), the dog receives both cues at...
sections.notes: Nearby marker: deep-dive__step-content No hosted asset candidates found in this section window.

sections.heading: Boundary Crossed
sections.subhead: If the dog passes the fence line, Emergency Feedback activates and continues as long as the dog is moving away from safety and remains within the Protection Zone (~300 feet beyond the fence).
sections.copySummary: If the dog passes the fence line, Emergency Feedback activates and continues as long as the dog is moving away from safety and remains within the Protection Zone (~300 feet beyond the fence). 5
sections.notes: Nearby marker: deep-dive__step-content No hosted asset candidates found in this section window.

sections.heading: Dog Turns Back
sections.copySummary: The moment the collar detects the dog has stopped or reversed direction, all prevention feedback stops immediately. Encouragement Feedback begins — the Whistle, Good Dog, or Go Home cue plays to guide the dog back to the safe zone. 6
sections.notes: Nearby marker: deep-dive__step-content No hosted asset candidates found in this section window.

sections.heading: Lost Mode
sections.copySummary: Emergency Feedback stops. The collar switches to the Return Whistle encouragement cue only, to guide the dog home without escalating confusion. You receive a push notification and can track the dog’s live location in the Halo app.
sections.notes: Nearby marker: deep-dive__step-content 1 hosted asset candidate(s).

sections.heading: Halo Uses Static Feedback — Not an Electric Shock
sections.copySummary: A common concern among dog owners is whether GPS fence collars deliver painful shocks. Halo’s static feedback is fundamentally different from the electric pulse used in traditional shock collars. Static feedback creates a sense of “touch” designed to mimic...
